name: Bulwark
flavor: The tank's discipline: a Sandow who plants himself between the crowd and the danger and simply refuses to move, soaking up every blow the show throws at him and daring anything to get past.

features.name: Iron Resolve
features.description: Your body is trained to feed off punishment, your own and the punishment aimed at those you're shielding. In addition to your baseline Grit generation, you gain 1 additional Grit the first time each turn you take damage from an attack, and you gain 1 Grit the first time each turn a hostile creature's attack hits an ally within 5 feet of you (hits on yourself are already covered by the first clause) — two separate sources, each capped at once per turn, exactly like your baseline Grit generation. Standing at the front and eating blows, yours or an ally's, is how a Bulwark keeps his Grit topped up.

features.name: Guardian's Wall
features.description: You make yourself the obstacle nothing gets past. Its protection works three ways:

options.name: Challenge (taunt)
options.effect: While you have at least 1 Grit, every enemy within 10 feet of you has disadvantage on any attack roll that does not include you as a target — swing at your allies and your bulk is always in the line. An enemy can still act freely by targeting you instead, or by stepping out of your 10-foot reach.

options.name: Intercept
options.effect: Once per turn, when a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can throw yourself into the blow — the attack's damage is dealt to you instead of the original target. This costs you nothing and does not use your reaction, so it never competes with Parry or Hold the Line. You may reduce the damage with Brace as normal, but you cannot Parry a hit you Intercept — it has already landed.

options.name: Braced Footing
options.effect: While you have at least 1 Grit, you have advantage on saving throws to avoid being knocked prone or forcibly moved.

features.name: Living Barricade
features.description: You are a wall that walks, and everyone who has ever tried to get past you knows it. Outside combat, you count as one size larger and have advantage on any check made to block a passage, hold a door or gate shut, anchor a rope or a person, or resist being moved or dragged. A doorway, corridor, or gap you choose to fill with your body cannot be squeezed past without your permission.
features.sheetSummary: Count as one size larger + advantage on checks to block passages, hold doors shut, anchor ropes/people, or resist being moved. A gap you fill can't be squeezed past without your consent.
